source: Curated from operator disclosures, regulator filings, GEM (CC-BY 4.0) + Global Energy Monitor (CC-BY 4.0)

operatorStatement.text: Germany ended Druzhba imports Jan 2023; PCK Schwedt now sourced via Rostock/Gdansk.

habshan-fujairah.name: Abu Dhabi Crude Oil Pipeline (Habshan–Fujairah)
habshan-fujairah.note: Strategic Hormuz bypass — rerouted volume during Hormuz disruptions.

kirkuk-ceyhan.name: Kirkuk–Ceyhan Pipeline
kirkuk-ceyhan.operator: BOTAS / North Oil Company / Iraqi State Oil Marketing Organization

evidence.note: Flow halted in Mar 2023 following ICC arbitration ruling; intermittent resumption since.

operatorStatement.text: TMX expansion commissioned May 2024, tripling capacity to 890 kbpd.

seaway.name: Seaway Pipeline
seaway.operator: Enbridge / Enterprise Products (Seaway Crude Pipeline Company)

operatorStatement.text: Pipeline closed since Iraqi invasion of Kuwait 1990; Saudi Arabia nationalised the line 2001 and converted the western section 2017 to carry natural gas.
Line 5 continues to operate; ongoing litigation with the State of Michigan over easement under Straits of Mackinac. Tunnel replacement project in permitting.
Biden administration revoked presidential permit 20 Jan 2021. TC Energy terminated the project 9 Jun 2021. Not constructed; listed for historical / geopolitical completeness.
Brent field in decommissioning phase; pipeline continues to serve residual Shetland North Sea production, throughput reduced from peak.

khafji-neutral-zone.name: Khafji–Neutral Zone Export Pipeline
khafji-neutral-zone.operator: KJO (Saudi Arabian Chevron / Kuwait Gulf Oil)

evidence.operatorStatement: Operations suspended 2014 over Saudi-Kuwait Neutral Zone operational dispute; partial restart late 2019 but throughput below nameplate per KJO operator statements + Reuters coverage.
Repeated conflict-driven shutdowns since 2011 South Sudan independence; most recent halt April 2023 per Sudan Ministry of Energy + Reuters coverage.

operatorStatement.text: Crude exports through the Transandino halted 2006 amid Argentine export restrictions; pipeline intermittent since. Some natural-gas liquids traffic continues.
Recurring force majeure declarations due to crude theft + pipeline vandalism in the Niger Delta; throughput significantly below nameplate.

nigeria-bonny-export.name: Nigeria Bonny Export System (Trans Niger Pipeline)

operatorStatement.text: Trans Niger Pipeline (TNP) chronically disrupted by tap-ins; Shell has declared force majeure multiple times in recent years. Bonny terminal exports intermittent.

Pipeline was closed by newly-independent Iraq in 1948 and abandoned; sections dismantled or repurposed. Listed for historical / geopolitical completeness; periodically floated as a reopening proposal.

uganda-tanzania-eacop-planned.name: East African Crude Oil Pipeline (EACOP, under construction)

operatorStatement.text: Under construction; first oil targeted 2025 (subject to continued schedule slips). Project faces financing challenges after Western bank withdrawals citing climate / human-rights concerns.

myanmar-china-crude.name: Myanmar–China Crude Pipeline
myanmar-china-crude.operator: South East Asia Crude Pipeline Company (CNPC-led)

operatorStatement.text: Chronic ELN insurgent sabotage attacks interrupt throughput multiple times annually; 2024 attacks again forced force-majeure declarations.
Recurring spill incidents + indigenous community protests have forced intermittent shutdowns across the jungle sections since 2016.

evidence.operatorStatement: PDVSA infrastructure degraded post-2017; throughput reported well below nameplate per S&P Global Platts + Bloomberg PDVSA coverage.

south-sudan-kenya-lamu-planned.name: South Sudan–Kenya Lamu Crude Pipeline (LAPSSET, planned)
south-sudan-kenya-lamu-planned.operator: South Sudan Ministry of Petroleum / LAPSSET Corridor Development Authority

operatorStatement.text: LAPSSET programme (Lamu Port South-Sudan Ethiopia Transport) includes a crude pipeline to reduce South Sudan dependence on Sudan transit. Financing + alignment remain unresolved. Construction not commenced on main trunk.

evidence.operatorStatement: Pemex Gulf pipelines face recurring leaks and clandestine tap theft (huachicol); throughput reduced since 2017 per Pemex annual reports + Reuters Mexico coverage.
